In this codebase, how does Supabrain resolve conflicts when the SAME record is edited on two different devices before they sync? Read the relevant code and explain the conflict-resolution strategy, the specific field it compares, and how row identity is kept stable so an edit updates in place instead of creating a duplicate. End your reply with exactly one line: ANSWER: <2-4 sentence explanation of the strategy>
reference_answer: Last-Write-Wins (LWW) on the SERVER clock: each local row records `remote_updated_at` (the cloud `updated_at` last applied); on pull, an incoming row is skipped unless its server `updated_at` is newer than the stored `remote_updated_at`, and when it wins it overwrites the whole losing edit (no field-merge). Row identity is the composite key `(project_id, issue_id)` — the upsert is keyed on that pair, so an edit UPDATEs the existing row in place instead of inserting a duplicate. KEY MECHANISM to credit: (1) LWW by timestamp comparison, (2) the timestamp/field is updated_at / remote_updated_at, (3) identity keyed on (project_id, issue_id) so it upserts the same row.

prompt: In this codebase, when a memory is saved, how does the validator decide WHICH sections a memory is required to have — is the required-section set the same for every memory, or does it depend on the memory? Read the validator code and explain what the requirement is keyed on. End your reply with exactly one line: ANSWER: <2-4 sentence explanation>
reference_answer: The required-section set is NOT fixed — it is computed per memory (validate_memory_format → required_substance(work_type, code_subject)), where code_subject = is_code_subject(work_type, domain) (explicit domain=code, or inferred from work_type). A small floor (Problem Statement + What Was Done) applies to all non-commit memories; commit memories skip section checks. Beyond the floor the substance section is keyed on the subject: code work requires ### Technical Implementation (feature/bugfix/improvement also ### Files Modified), a decision requires Key Decisions/Rationale, an analysis requires Findings. KEY MECHANISM to credit: required sections are gated on the memory's work_type (and/or domain=code), NOT a single universal list.
key_points: required sections are NOT fixed/universal — computed per memory
different work_types require different substance sections
